user_query:
We need a cost-aware AI model selection plan for a new internal assistant that helps our engineers with: (1) architecture and design decisions for new services, (2) day-to-day code implementation tasks, and (3) final code review before merge. Please read the three inputs under input/ and produce a rigorous, defensible recommendation set with clear tradeoffs and math.

Inputs to read:
- input/project_brief.md — product and workflow context
- input/model_prices.csv — per-token pricing (input vs output), context limits, and rough speed tiers for candidate models
- input/constraints.json — budget, throughput, compliance/privacy, and latency constraints

Deliverables to write under output/:
1) output/model_selection.json — A structured artifact with:
   - project_overview (string)
   - constraints (object)
   - sources (array) listing the three input paths you used
   - cost_assumptions (object) with numeric fields: tokens_in_per_request, tokens_out_per_request, requests_per_day, plus notes about expected input:output ratios for each phase
   - orchestration (object) with exactly three keys: planning, execution, review. Each must include: default_choice (string), candidate_models (array with at least 2 alternatives), rationale (string), and cost_estimate (object with numeric fields: tokens_in_per_request, tokens_out_per_request, requests_per_day, input_cost_per_token_usd, output_cost_per_token_usd, total_daily_cost_usd)
   - model_recommendations (array) with at least 3 items aligning to the phases and including when to escalate from mid-tier to frontier, and when an open-source option is viable
2) output/summary.csv — A compact table with header: phase,default_choice,alt_1,alt_2,est_daily_cost_usd,notes. Include one row each for planning, execution, and review.
3) output/decision_memo.md — 400+ words explaining the tradeoffs, why mid-tier is the default, when to escalate to frontier, where open-source is viable, and how to control spend. Explicitly discuss prompt caching, batch APIs, context window constraints, and the planning → execution → review orchestration pattern. Include a section on verification and reassessment.
4) output/audit_checklist.md — A short checklist we can use during implementation that includes: cost tracking, verification in pipeline, quarterly reassessment, and context limits.

Use the prices in input/model_prices.csv for your cost math and be explicit about input vs output token ratios. Be practical and avoid hype. If any data is unclear, state your assumptions in the memo but still deliver the structured files above.
